entities.notes: Real CRUD entity. read_schema=CustomEventResponse wraps custom_event (CustomEventModel) which has required integer 'id'; path param customevent_id maps to id. POST creates, PUT updates, DELETE deletes (EmptyResponse). create_req_schema is an inline urlencoded 'FormParams' object (fields: alternatives, name); not a named component schema so no resolvable component name. jsonencode confirmed: custom_event.alternatives[].valid_segfilter is anyOf[object(additionalProperties:true), null]. No collapse_fields: remaining anyOf cases (deleted, user_id, serialized) are nullable single scalars. Singularized custom_events->custom_event.

Real CRUD entity. Collection GET/POST, instance GET (BaseOkResponseModel_ServiceAccountModel_) + DELETE. ServiceAccountModel has a direct integer id -> id_attr=id. create_req_schema=CreateServiceAccountRequest. No update endpoint -> update null. anyOf fields (creator, creator_email, expires, last_used, role) are all single-scalar-with-null, not >=2 distinct scalars and not object/dynamic, so no collapse_fields or jsonencode_fields. Sibling endpoints (add-to-project, delete-service-accounts, remove-from-project, add-to-team, remove-from-team) are RPC actions, not this resource.

Real CRUD entity: POST(AgentFlowCreatePayload)/GET/PATCH(AgentFlowUpdatePayload)/DELETE; typed read schema AgentFlowApiResponse (inside BaseOkResponseModel_AgentFlowApiResponse_ envelope). id_param agent_flow_id maps to AgentFlowApiResponse.id (uuid4 string). Repaired jsonencode_fields: candidate 'frequency' is WRONG — AgentFlowFrequency is a plain string enum (hour/day/week/month), a scalar. The dynamic field is 'graph' ($ref AgentFlowGraph; nodes = discriminated oneOf of TriggerNode/MetricAnalysisNode/NotificationNode/BranchNode) -> jsonencode; graph is also in create payload. collapse_fields empty: every entity anyOf is 'type | null' (nullable single scalar). status/type are plain enums.

Real user-manageable CRUD entity. POST create (CreateCustomAlertRequest), GET list+entry, PATCH update (UpdateCustomAlertRequest), DELETE. read_schema CustomAlertResponse exposes typed 'id' (and 'name'); open/extra=allow tail but stable identity so id_attr='id'. jsonencode_fields: 'condition' is oneOf(AlertConditionNormal|AlertConditionAnomaly); 'subscriptions' is array of discriminated oneOf (email/slack_webhook/slack_channel/project_webhook). 'notification_windows' is a plain nullable object ref (single concrete type), left nested. No multi-scalar anyOf -> collapse_fields empty.

Real CRUD entity. read_schema AnnotationResponse is a typed entity body with integer 'id' (path param annotation_id -> id_attr id). create POST requestBody resolves allOf->AnnotationCreateRequest. Full CRUD on instance path (get/put/patch/delete). jsonencode_fields=['user'] confirmed: user is anyOf[AnnotationUser object, null] (nullable object ref). No collapse_fields: no anyOf of >=2 plain scalars. 'tags' is a typed array of AnnotationTagRef objects, representable as a TF nested list, left as-is.

Real user-manageable CRUD entity (saved bookmarks/reports). read_schema BookmarkApiResponse is a typed entity (not *_dict_ untyped) with integer 'id' that bookmark_id maps to. Create=POST PostBookmarkRequest; collection GET lists. Candidate said update=put, but PUT on the instance has NO requestBody — the real typed update is PATCH (PatchBookmarkRequest, all-optional). Corrected update to 'patch'. metadata and params are anyOf:[{}, null] (untyped/dynamic JSON objects) -> jsonencode. No anyOf-of-multiple-plain-scalars, so no collapse_fields. Note: response schema is additionalProperties:true (permissive docs-only model) but is still typed with concrete properties, so it is a valid entity body.

Real user-manageable CRUD entity (canvas / metric tree). Full CRUD: POST collection=CanvasCreateParams, PATCH=CanvasUpdateParams, GET/DELETE instance. read_schema CanvasResponse typed with required id (string) + name/description/creator/timestamp fields. id_param=metric_tree_id but entity exposes plain id -> id_attr=id. Singularized canvases->canvas. No oneOf/discriminated/anyOf-object -> no jsonencode_fields; all anyOf are nullable-scalar -> no collapse_fields.

Real CRUD entity. read_schema CohortResponse has integer 'id' (required) so id_attr=id even though path param is cohort_id. create POST body=CohortCreateRequest. Corrected update verb from candidate 'put' to 'patch': PUT has no requestBody; PATCH carries typed CohortPatchRequest. collapse_fields data_group_id=anyOf[integer,string,null] (two plain scalars) confirmed. jsonencode_fields added 'groups' (anyOf[array of dynamic objects,null] in CohortResponse; create/patch also carry dynamic groups/behaviors/selector empty-schema). Also exposed under /api/app/workspaces/{workspace_id}/cohorts.

Real CRUD entity (data pipeline connector). GET returns BaseOkResponseModel_Connector_ wrapping typed Connector in results, so read_schema=Connector. create=POST /api/app/projects/{project_id}/connectors with body CreateConnectorRequestModel. delete=DELETE on /api/app/projects/{project_id}/connectors/{connector_id}. No update endpoint (update=null). id_attr=connector_id: Connector has no 'id' but exposes connector_id matching the {connector_id} path param. jsonencode: kept connector_properties (free-form additionalProperties:true object) and category_properties (CategoryProperties has polymorphic member 'events' string|array, safer as JSON string). DROPPED created_by from jsonencode: UserInfo is a fixed-shape flat typed object (id/name/email scalars), not dynamic, so does not qualify. collapse_fields empty: all entity anyOf are scalar+null nullable, not 2+ plain scalars. (collection/instance fields echoed as resource short name since schema only allows scalar strings.)

Real user-manageable CRUD entity. Full POST(create)/GET(read+list)/PUT(update)/DELETE. Typed entity body CustomRoleResponse (not *_dict_ untyped). id_param role_id maps to read_schema attribute role_id (uuid; no plain 'id'). create_req_schema=CustomRoleCreatePayload (direct $ref). create verb=post. No jsonencode_fields: anyOf props (created_by_user_id, project_id, permissions, copied_from_role_id) are nullable wrappers (X|null), not oneOf/discriminated/dynamic objects. No collapse_fields: no anyOf of >=2 distinct plain scalars.

Full CRUD entity. POST /custom_properties body CustomPropertyCreateRequest; GET/PUT/DELETE on /{custom_property_id}; PUT body allOf[CustomPropertyUpdateRequest]. Read schema CustomPropertyResponse; no plain id, path param maps to integer customPropertyId. jsonencode_fields behavior/composedProperties/displayOptions are anyOf[object,null] dynamic objects. No collapse_fields.

Real CRUD entity. read_schema EmailDigestResponse has required typed integer `id`; path param email_digest_id maps to entity attribute id. Full lifecycle: POST create (EmailDigestCreateRequest), PUT update, DELETE, GET. slack_subscriptions = anyOf[array of dynamic additionalProperties:true objects, null] -> jsonencode. No anyOf-of-multiple-scalars, so collapse_fields empty. Singularized email_digests -> email_digest.

Real CRUD entity. Full lifecycle: POST collection (create), GET/PATCH/DELETE instance. read_schema DashboardResponse is a typed entity body (wrapped BaseOkResponseModel_DashboardResponse_); required 'id' is a plain integer so id_attr=id, mapped from path param dashboard_id. Create request body resolves allOf -> PostDashboardRequest. No collapse_fields: every anyOf property is exactly one scalar plus null (e.g. description string|null, creator_id integer|null), no >=2 distinct scalar union. No jsonencode_fields: no oneOf/discriminated or anyOf-with-object/array members. Singularized dashboards->dashboard.

PROMOTED TO RESOURCE (P1 gap report requirement). Lexicon tag resource for categorizing events and properties in the schema governance system. API exposes GET/POST/PATCH but NO DELETE (would 405). delete=null means Terraform will remove from state without API call, orphaning the tag - this is acceptable for metadata tags. Used in event_definition.tags and custom_property.tags arrays. Fields: id (computed integer), name (required string), description (optional string), color (optional string). Renamed from 'tag' to 'lexicon_tag' to clarify purpose and avoid generic naming.

Real user-manageable CRUD entity. Collection POST + instance GET/PATCH/DELETE all present; the many extra subpaths (archive/decide/launch/duplicate/force_conclude/etc.) are RPC actions and not part of the resource. Entity body is typed (ExperimentApiResponse) with a real string `id` property, so id_attr=id even though the path param is experiment_id. Create requestBody is the typed ExperimentCreatePayload (resolved from $ref). Singularized resource_name to 'experiment'. jsonencode_fields: kept exposures_cache, feature_flag, results_cache (anyOf object[additionalProperties:true]/null = dynamic objects), settings (anyOf $ref ExperimentSettings object/null), and metrics.definition (anyOf object[additionalProperties:true]/null). DROPPED 'status' from jsonencode_fields: it is anyOf [ExperimentStatus enum string, null] — a plain scalar enum, not an object/oneOf, so it should be a normal string attribute, not jsonencode. No collapse_fields: no attribute has anyOf of >=2 plain scalars.

Real CRUD entity: POST/GET/PUT/PATCH/DELETE all present. read_schema FeatureFlagApiResponse is typed (wrapped server-side in BaseOkResponseModel_FeatureFlagApiResponse_) and has a string 'id', so id_attr=id maps flag_id. create POST body=FeatureFlagApiPayload (PUT shares it; PATCH uses FeatureFlagApiPatchPayload). All jsonencode_fields verified: content_environments (anyOf ref+null), ruleset.test (anyOf dynamic nested map+null), ruleset.variants.value (anyOf boolean/string/dynamic-object), ruleset.rollout.{cohort_definition,runtime_evaluation_definition,runtime_evaluation_rule} (anyOf additionalProperties-object+null), ruleset.rollout.{runtime_event_rule,variant_override} (anyOf object-ref+null). ruleset.rollout is array of Rollout; dotted item paths correct. collapse_fields content_environments_id verified: anyOf integer/string/null -> single scalar. Spec: /home/joshua_koehler/analytics/webapp/ninja/generated/openapi.merged.json

Real CRUD entity: POST collection / GET-PUT-DELETE instance. Response schema HeatMapCollectionApiResponse has typed fields and an 'id' (uuid4) that the path param heat_map_collection_id maps to. Create body is HeatMapCollectionApiPayload (direct $ref, required name). 'heat_maps' is an array of anyOf{HeatMapApiPayload, HeatMapApiResponse} (polymorphic object array) -> jsonencode. Remaining anyOf fields are all nullable single-scalars (type+null), not multi-scalar collapse candidates, so collapse_fields empty.

Real user-manageable CRUD entity. POST create_metric(MetricsRequest), GET list+entry, PATCH update_metric(MetricPatch instance), DELETE delete_metric(instance). read_schema typed (Metric entity), not untyped dict. Metric is discriminated oneOf (BehaviorMetric/FormulaMetric, propertyName=type); both variants require id:integer so id_attr=id (path param metric_id maps to entity id). create_req_schema=MetricsRequest (oneOf BehaviorMetricRequest/FormulaMetricRequest). PROMOTED data-source->resource: tfplugingen-openapi cannot model the oneOf roots (MetricsRequest / Metric) -> 'unsupported multi-type, attribute cannot be created'. preprocess_spec repoints the metric create body to concrete_create_schema (BehaviorMetricRequest) and all metric 2xx responses to concrete_read_schema (BehaviorMetric); the two variants are field-identical except for `definition` (which is ignored/jsonencode'd), so the collapsed concrete schema is lossless. The wire response is enveloped + a single-entry id->object map (results_map), handled in crudgen. jsonencode: 'definition' is variant-specific complex nested object ($ref BehaviorMetricDefinition/FormulaMetricDefinition) -> dynamic. No collapse_fields: all anyOf are scalar+null (nullability only). owned_by/created_by/last_verified_by are typed single User refs.

Formula metric CRUD entity. Shares /metrics endpoint with behavior metrics but uses FormulaMetric discriminator type. POST create_metric(FormulaMetricRequest with type='formula'), GET/PATCH/DELETE on instance. Discriminated from BehaviorMetric by type field. create_req_schema=MetricsRequest (oneOf wrapper), concrete schema is FormulaMetricRequest. read_schema BaseOkResponseModel_Dict_str__Metric__ envelope, concrete schema is FormulaMetric. id_attr=id. jsonencode: 'definition' is FormulaMetricDefinition (nested formula expression + referencedMetrics). bulk_delete_only: single-item DELETE returns 501, must use bulk DELETE endpoint. Custom validation required: referencedMetrics must map to contiguous variables A, B, C... in order.

Real user-manageable CRUD entity (Session Replay playlists). Full CRUD present. read_schema PlaylistApiResponse has typed uuid 'id' (id_attr=id; playlist_id maps to id). create_req_schema=PlaylistApiPayload (required: name). jsonencode_fields confirmed: filters (anyOf[PlaylistFilters nested obj of dynamic additionalProperties:true arrays, null]) and time_filter (anyOf[free-form object, null]). No collapse_fields: all anyOf are X-or-null.

Real CRUD entity (POST/GET/PATCH/DELETE) for project themes. read_schema candidate was ThemesResultsMap, but that is just a map wrapper (additionalProperties -> ThemeResponse); the actual typed entity is ThemeResponse, which has a typed integer 'id' that the path param theme_id maps to. create_req_schema is ThemeCreateRequest (required: name, type[const 'categorical'], data[ThemeData]; optional global_access_type enum). PATCH body is ThemePatchRequest (all optional). jsonencode 'data': in ThemeResponse the 'data' property is an untyped free-form object (additionalProperties:true / dynamic), so it must be surfaced as a jsonencode string for stable TF state; the create body's data (ThemeData) is structured but maps to the same dynamic entity field. No collapse_fields: global_access_type is anyOf[enum-string, null] (single scalar + null), not >=2 distinct plain scalars.

Real user-manageable WHC warehouse source. Full CRUD (POST create, GET read, PATCH update, DELETE). Read entity WarehouseSourceResponse is flat with integer primary key `id` (path param source_id -> id). Renamed sources -> warehouse_source (singular; feature is warehouse-sources, plain 'source' is ambiguous).

IMPORTANT for codegen: both POST create and PATCH update bodies are DISCRIMINATED oneOf unions keyed by `warehouse_type` (snowflake/bigquery/redshift/databricks/postgres), with per-variant schemas *SourceCreate / *SourcePatch. There is no single flat create schema. create_req_schema is set to SnowflakeSourceCreate as a representative variant, but the request body is polymorphic. Variant create schemas: BigQuerySourceCreate, SnowflakeSourceCreate, RedshiftSourceCreate, DatabricksSourceCreate, PostgresSourceCreate (analogous *SourcePatch for update).

Read entity has no oneOf/anyOf-object fields and no >=2-scalar anyOf unions (anyOf fields are all scalar|null nullables), so no entity-relative jsonencode_fields or collapse_fields apply.

Real user-manageable CRUD entity. Full lifecycle: POST collection (create_rollup_project, body UpsertRollupProjectRequest); GET instance (get_rollup_project); POST instance (upsert_rollup_project) is UPDATE with same UpsertRollupProjectRequest body -> repaired candidate update:null to update:post; DELETE instance. read_schema GetRollupProjectsResponse is a container map; real entity is RollupProjectEntry (keyed by stringified id under rollupProjects) with integer 'id' -> id_attr=id matches path param rollup_project_id. create_req_schema=UpsertRollupProjectRequest (direct $ref). No jsonencode_fields (all props nullable scalars/string-array, no oneOf/discriminated/dynamic objects). No collapse_fields (no anyOf of >=2 plain scalars; only T-or-null nullables).

KEEP: real user-manageable SCIM Group (team) entity with full CRUD. collection=/api/appscim/v2/Groups (POST create, body ScimGroupCreateRequest with displayName required), instance=/api/appscim/v2/Groups/{group_scim_id} (GET read, PUT update, DELETE). read_schema ScimGroupResource is a serialized team singleton (additionalProperties:true; only 'schemas' string[] declared explicitly). id_attr='id' is the SCIM canonical identifier (read schema is open so id is returned at runtime); if the generator requires a field declared in the schema, fall back to the path param 'group_scim_id'. PUT declares no requestBody in the spec; update body is effectively the same SCIM group shape as create. No oneOf/discriminated or anyOf-with-object/dynamic fields on the read entity itself (members[list[dict]] and meta[object] live only on ScimGroupCreateRequest, not the read entity), so jsonencode_fields and collapse_fields are both empty. resource_name singularized to scim_group to disambiguate from a possible 'team' resource.

P2 read-from-list: no instance GET. create=POST collection (WebhookCreatePayload), update=PATCH instance (WebhookUpdatePayload), delete=DELETE instance. Read selects the matching WebhookItem from the collection GET (BaseOkResponseModel_List_WebhookItem__). id_attr=id maps to {webhook_id} path param. All scalar fields (auth_type/name/url/username/password) -> no jsonencode. Resource-only (no instance GET to back a singular data source).

P2 read-from-list: no instance GET. create=POST collection (CreateDataGroupRequest), update=POST instance (RenameDataGroupRequest), delete=DELETE instance. Read selects matching DataGroup from collection GET (BaseOkResponseModel_List_DataGroup__). id_attr=data_group_id equals the {data_group_id} path param. metadata is a typed nested object (DataGroupMetadata, all nullable scalars) -> no jsonencode. Resource-only.

P2 CRUD entity. POST collection (EventDefinitionCreateRequest, body wraps a 'definitions' array batch), GET/PATCH(EventDefinitionPatchRequest)/DELETE instance. read_schema EventDefinitionResult has required integer 'id' -> id_attr=id (path param event_definition_id templates URL). collection POST returns BaseOkResponseModel_List_dict__ (untyped list) while instance GET returns BaseOkResponseModel_EventDefinitionResult_; the schema merge uses the create REQUEST root + read RESPONSE root, both clean. No oneOf/dynamic-object props -> no jsonencode_fields; array props (contacts/platforms/tags/team_contacts) are typed arrays.

P2 CRUD entity. POST collection (WorkspaceCreateRequest), GET/PATCH(WorkspacePatchRequest)/DELETE instance. read_schema MeWorkspace has required number 'id' -> id_attr=id (path param workspace_id templates URL). jsonencode 'data_filters' is anyOf[object,null] dynamic object. project_id is a returned scalar (number) and also the URL scope; the bridge keeps the scope attr out of the body. member_list/team_list/etc are nullable typed arrays.

P2 client-id UPSERT entity. dataset_id is CLIENT-SUPPLIED (read_schema Dataset has required string 'dataset_id', no plain 'id'); id_attr=dataset_id. Collection GET lists only (no POST). Instance: GET read, POST upsert (DatasetUpsertRequest) for BOTH create and update, DELETE delete. Create and update both POST to the instance path -> crudgen OVERRIDES create_to_instance=True + update=post. No oneOf/dynamic props -> no jsonencode_fields.

DATA-SOURCE-ONLY (integrated_partial). read_schema ProjectOutgoingIntegration has required integer 'id'. Read instance GET is /api/app/projects/{project_id}/integrations/outgoing/{project_outgoing_integration_id}. There is NO DELETE verb anywhere for this entity; deletion is POST /api/app/projects/{project_id}/integrations/{integration_name}/disconnect, which keys off integration_name (NOT the tracked numeric id), so delete cannot be cleanly mapped from the id we hold. Registering this as a create-able resource would create UNREMOVABLE orphans, so it is exposed only as a singular data source. jsonencode 'settings' = anyOf[object,null]. status/workflowState/createdBy/cohorts are typed objects/arrays/enum-string.

DATA-SOURCE-ONLY. Read-only project schema graph data source. GET /data-definitions/schema returns SchemaResult (dynamic object containing events array with density_local metrics). No create/update/delete operations - purely read-only for visualizing unified event-property graph with density metrics. Used for schema governance and drift-checking. id_attr=project_id since this is a project-scoped singleton. jsonencode 'events' because SchemaResult is additionalProperties:true dynamic object.

Collection-path body-id CRUD: GET/POST/PATCH/DELETE all live on the collection path; the id is carried in the JSON body (DELETE also takes a body). create POST collection (no id), response is enveloped list[EventDropFilterResult]; the created filter is selected from the list by matching event_name (newest=max id). read GET collection then filter by body id (read_from_list). update PATCH collection with id injected into body. delete DELETE collection with id in body. read_schema EventDropFilterResult; create_req_schema EventDropFilterRequest. filters is a free-form list[object] -> jsonencode passthrough.

Project SINGLETON: no collection POST-with-server-id, no id path segment. synthetic id = project_id. create=update=POST /data-governance/data-standards/settings (body DataStandardsSettingsRequest). read=GET (returns DataStandards root; runtime wraps it under dataStandards to match the request schema). delete=no-op (remove from state, do NOT mutate). read_schema/create_req_schema=DataStandardsSettingsRequest (single dataStandards nested object treated as jsonencode passthrough string). The GET 2xx response is repointed to DataStandardsSettingsRequest in preprocess so the framework read schema == request schema.

Project SINGLETON: business context markdown storage. No collection POST-with-server-id, no id path segment. synthetic id = project_id. create=update=PUT /api/app/projects/{project_id}/business-context (stores markdown content). read=GET /api/app/projects/{project_id}/business-context (retrieves org + project business context). delete=no-op (remove from state, do NOT mutate). This is a singleton resource containing organization and project-level business context in markdown format. The GET 2xx response is repointed to BusinessContextRequest in preprocess so the framework read schema == request schema.

Net-new behaviors CRUD. The collection POST create_behaviors accepts a POLYMORPHIC body (a single behavior {name,description,type,definition} OR a bulk {behaviors:[...]}) and ALL responses (list/get/create/update) are the UNTYPED BaseOkResponseModel[dict] envelope (results is an open object) -- there is no typed read entity in the spec. We therefore repoint the collection POST + instance GET + instance PATCH 2xx responses to the request schema BehaviorCreateRequest (read_repoint_schema) so the framework builds the resource schema from the request fields (name/description/type + the polymorphic definition/behaviors). definition (BehaviorDefinition, open) and behaviors (list[BehaviorCreateItem]) are jsonencode passthroughs. create POST returns an untyped envelope whose results carries the new id; read_after_create extracts id_attr=id from the create response then GETs the instance (behavior_id maps to entity id). update PATCH update_behavior on the instance path (BehaviorUpdateRequest superset is body-compatible). delete DELETE delete_behavior on the instance path. SINGLE-behavior modeling: a tf resource manages one behavior; supply name/type and optional definition JSON. If the live create response nests the id under results.behavior.id or results.id, adjust id_attr / ID_JSON_PATH accordingly (verify against project 4035291).

org-scoped SETTINGS SINGLETON. READ=GET /organizations/{organization_id}/org-request-access-settings (loose/untyped result, additionalProperties=true); WRITE=POST /organizations/{organization_id}/org-request-access-settings/update (NO request body in spec). Modeled as a single `settings` jsonencode passthrough holding the whole settings object. synthetic id = org id. read repointed to MxpSettingsPassthrough (uniform {settings:string}) in preprocess; `settings` ignored in config and re-injected as a jsonencode string attr. create==update POST the decoded settings object to update_path; delete=no-op. settings_passthrough=True sends the unwrapped settings object (not {settings:...}) as the body.

org-scoped SETTINGS SINGLETON. READ=GET /organizations/{organization_id}/org-session-settings (loose/untyped result, additionalProperties=true); WRITE=POST /organizations/{organization_id}/org-session-settings/update (NO request body in spec). Modeled as a single `settings` jsonencode passthrough holding the whole settings object. synthetic id = org id. read repointed to MxpSettingsPassthrough (uniform {settings:string}) in preprocess; `settings` ignored in config and re-injected as a jsonencode string attr. create==update POST the decoded settings object to update_path; delete=no-op. settings_passthrough=True sends the unwrapped settings object (not {settings:...}) as the body.

ORG-scoped RPC lifecycle. Project has no typed REST CRUD: create=POST /organizations/{organization_id}/create-projects/ (body {projectNames:[name]}), read=GET /organizations/{organization_id}/projects/ (enveloped results is an ARRAY of serialized org projects; select by id), delete=POST /organizations/{organization_id}/delete-projects/ (body {projectIds:[id]}). No typed request/response bodies in the merged spec (OrgProjectsResponse is an open object); a synthetic ProjectLifecycleModel schema (name settable; id/token/api_key/api_secret/url computed outputs) is injected in preprocess_spec.py and both create POST requestBody + list GET 2xx response are repointed to it. No update endpoint -> name is ForceNew. transfer-project / projects/reset / reset_keys are sibling RPC actions, intentionally not modeled here (transfer mutates org membership; reset is destructive data-wipe). create/delete mutate the REAL org.

org-scoped SETTINGS SINGLETON. READ=GET /organizations/{organization_id}/spark-settings (loose/untyped result, additionalProperties=true); WRITE=POST /organizations/{organization_id}/update-spark-settings (NO request body in spec). Modeled as a single `settings` jsonencode passthrough holding the whole settings object. synthetic id = org id. read repointed to MxpSettingsPassthrough (uniform {settings:string}) in preprocess; `settings` ignored in config and re-injected as a jsonencode string attr. create==update POST the decoded settings object to update_path; delete=no-op. settings_passthrough=True sends the unwrapped settings object (not {settings:...}) as the body.

entities.key_doc: the team name as it appears in the org teams list, or the team_id for update routing
entities.notes: RPC ASSOCIATION (rpc_assoc capability). The Mixpanel RBAC team surface is org-scoped RPC verbs with UNTYPED request bodies and an untyped (additionalProperties) list response, so tfplugingen-openapi skips it ('no compatible schema found') and it never reaches the IR. crudgen emits it fully self-contained (synthetic schema package internal/provider/resource_team + a typed-model resource internal/provider/team_resource.go). create=POST add-teams (body=jsonencode payload, the {teams:[...]} request shape); read=GET teams list and confirm the managed row by `key` (team name); update=POST teams/{key}/update (key templates the team_id segment); delete=POST delete-teams (body=jsonencode payload). synthetic id='<organization_id>:<key>'. The request/response bodies are untyped in the merged spec (OrgTeamsResponse/UpdatedResponse have additionalProperties:true, no properties), hence the jsonencode `payload` passthrough.

org-scoped SETTINGS SINGLETON. READ=GET /organizations/{organization_id}/twofactor (loose/untyped result, additionalProperties=true); WRITE=POST /organizations/{organization_id}/twofactor/update (NO request body in spec). Modeled as a single `settings` jsonencode passthrough holding the whole settings object. synthetic id = org id. read repointed to MxpSettingsPassthrough (uniform {settings:string}) in preprocess; `settings` ignored in config and re-injected as a jsonencode string attr. create==update POST the decoded settings object to update_path; delete=no-op. settings_passthrough=True sends the unwrapped settings object (not {settings:...}) as the body.

entities.key_doc: the user's email address, which must appear in the org users list (GET /organizations/{organization_id}/users/); Read confirms the grant by finding this email in that list
entities.notes: RPC ASSOCIATION grant (rpc_assoc capability), ForceNew. Associates a user with project(s) at a role. create=POST add-users-to-projects (UNTYPED body via jsonencode `payload`, e.g. {users:[...],projects:[...],role:...}); delete=POST delete-users (UNTYPED body via `payload`). No update verb -> key+payload+organization_id all RequiresReplace. read=GET the org USERS list (list_path) and confirm the grant still exists by `key` (the user's email). list_path was previously a copy-paste of the teams endpoint, which never contains the grant key -> every refresh removed the resource from state and recreated it (permanent drift). The /users/ list shape is open (additionalProperties); existence is confirmed by an exact recursive scan for the email. NOTE: /users/ cannot distinguish per-project grants, so existence is at user granularity. synthetic id='<organization_id>:<key>'. Bodies untyped in the merged spec (add-users-to-projects req: NONE; resp BaseOkResponseModel_SuccessResponse_).
